verses.text: Judge not, lest ye be judged; first cast the beam from thine own eye before the mote from thy brother's. Self first. Deal with your own faults before correcting others'.
Judge not, lest ye be judged; first cast the beam from thine own eye before the mote from thy brother's. Self first. Deal with your own faults before correcting others'.
Judge not, lest ye be judged; first cast the beam from thine own eye before the mote from thy brother's. Self first. Deal with your own faults before correcting others'.
Judge not, lest ye be judged; first cast the beam from thine own eye before the mote from thy brother's. Self first. Deal with your own faults before correcting others'.
Judge not, lest ye be judged; first cast the beam from thine own eye before the mote from thy brother's. Self first. Deal with your own faults before correcting others'.
Give not 'that which is holy unto the dogs,' nor cast pearls before swine. Discernment. Holy things are not to be wasted on the scornful.
'Ask, and it shall be given... seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened.' Persistent prayer. God answers those who keep on coming to Him.
'Ask, and it shall be given... seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened.' Persistent prayer. God answers those who keep on coming to Him.
If earthly fathers give good gifts, 'how much more shall your Father... give good things to them that ask?' The Father's heart. God delights to give what is truly good.
If earthly fathers give good gifts, 'how much more shall your Father... give good things to them that ask?' The Father's heart. God delights to give what is truly good.
If earthly fathers give good gifts, 'how much more shall your Father... give good things to them that ask?' The Father's heart. God delights to give what is truly good.
'All things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them.' The golden rule. Love's whole duty is summed in treating others as we wish to be treated.
Enter the strait gate; broad is the road to destruction, narrow the way to life, and few find it. Two ways. The path of life is hard, and the crowd does not walk it.
Enter the strait gate; broad is the road to destruction, narrow the way to life, and few find it. Two ways. The path of life is hard, and the crowd does not walk it.
Beware false prophets in sheep's clothing; 'by their fruits ye shall know them.' Test the teachers. A man's life reveals what his words conceal.
Beware false prophets in sheep's clothing; 'by their fruits ye shall know them.' Test the teachers. A man's life reveals what his words conceal.
Beware false prophets in sheep's clothing; 'by their fruits ye shall know them.' Test the teachers. A man's life reveals what his words conceal.
A good tree cannot bring evil fruit, nor an evil tree good; thus by their fruits they are known. Fruit tells. Character shows itself plainly in conduct.
A good tree cannot bring evil fruit, nor an evil tree good; thus by their fruits they are known. Fruit tells. Character shows itself plainly in conduct.
A good tree cannot bring evil fruit, nor an evil tree good; thus by their fruits they are known. Fruit tells. Character shows itself plainly in conduct.
Not 'Lord, Lord,' but doing the Father's will enters the kingdom; to some He says, 'I never knew you.' Empty profession. Words and works without true faith cannot save.
Not 'Lord, Lord,' but doing the Father's will enters the kingdom; to some He says, 'I never knew you.' Empty profession. Words and works without true faith cannot save.
Not 'Lord, Lord,' but doing the Father's will enters the kingdom; to some He says, 'I never knew you.' Empty profession. Words and works without true faith cannot save.
Whoso hears and does His sayings builds on rock; the careless builds on sand, and great is the fall. Doing, not hearing. Only obeyed truth gives a sure foundation.
Whoso hears and does His sayings builds on rock; the careless builds on sand, and great is the fall. Doing, not hearing. Only obeyed truth gives a sure foundation.

Whoso hears and does His sayings builds on rock; the careless builds on sand, and great is the fall. Doing, not hearing. Only obeyed truth gives a sure foundation.
The people are astonished, for He taught 'as one having authority, and not as the scribes.' Divine authority. Christ's word carries a weight no mere teacher had.
